title: Planning the Party
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct simple future statements using both 'Will' and 'Going to', demonstrating mastery of the bare infinitive rule and utilizing phonologic contractions ('ll).
instructions: You are planning a big party with your friend Sam. Sam is asking you what you will do to help. Reply ALOUD to his questions. Use 'I will' or 'I am going to'. Remember: Do NOT use any 'ing' or 'ed' endings after WILL!

contentItems.text: For extra points, try to squish 'I will' into 'I'll' when you speak!

speakingPrompt: Respond to the Hindi prompts aloud.
scenario: Planning a weekend party with a friend.

subTasks.prompt: सैम: 'We need food for the party.' (पार्टी के लिए हमें खाने की ज़रूरत है।) जवाब दें: 'मैं पिज़्ज़ा खरीदूँगा।' (I will...)
सैम: 'And what about drinks?' (और पीने के लिए क्या?) जवाब दें: 'मेरा भाई पानी लाएगा।' (My brother will...)
सैम: 'Who is going to clean the house?' (घर की सफाई कौन करने वाला है?) जवाब दें: 'हम घर साफ करने वाले हैं।' (We are going to...)

sampleResponse.targetText: We are going to clean the house.
sampleResponse.transliteration: वी आर गोइंग टू क्लीन द हाउस।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: हम घर साफ करने वाले हैं।

subTasks.prompt: सैम: 'Will it rain tomorrow?' (क्या कल बारिश होगी?) जवाब दें: 'नहीं, धूप होगी।' (No, it will...)
सैम: 'Great. It is going to be fun!' (बढ़िया। बहुत मज़ा आने वाला है!) जवाब दें: 'हाँ, हम मज़ा करेंगे!' (Yes, we will...)

scenarioContext.targetText: We need food for the party.
scenarioContext.transliteration: वी नीड फूड फॉर द पार्टी।
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: पार्टी के लिए हमें खाने की ज़रूरत है।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: We are going to clean the house.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: वी आर गोइंग टू क्लीन द हाउस।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हम घर साफ करने वाले हैं।

scoringRubric.No Suffixed Verbs: Did the user say 'I will buy' and NOT 'I will buying'?
scoringRubric.No Double Verbs: Did the user say 'I will buy' and NOT 'I am will buy'?
scoringRubric.Contraction Bonus: Did the user naturally contract 'I will' to 'I'll'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I'll buy pizza. My brother will bring water. We are going to clean the house. No, it'll be sunny. Yes, we'll have fun!

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You successfully predicted the future. More importantly, you kept all of your action words completely clean after saying 'Will'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! A very common mistake is saying 'We are will clean'. That is totally wrong. Just say 'We will clean'. Remove the 'are'!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you don't add 's' to the verb. Even if it is 'My brother', you DO NOT say 'My brother will brings'. It must be 'My brother will bring'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Sam: We need food for the party...

hintText: To speak future tense perfectly, remember that WILL acts like a shield. It protects the verb from getting any extra letters added to it!
